actions.title:
Curriculum and
Materials
actions.description:
Provide curriculum and materials to support teaching and learning TK-3:
* Provide standards-aligned materials in TK-3, including new sets of
decodable texts
* Provide supplemental materials for intervention and enrichment

actions.title:
Responsive and
Aligned Professional
Development
actions.description:
LREBG Action
Research indicates that high-quality professional development for
educators in the early grades, coupled with ongoing collaboration, is
essential for closing achievement gaps among English Language
Learners, foster youth, and low-income students (Darling-Hammond et al.,
2017). With this in mind, during the 2025-2026 school year, USD will
provide grade-level collaboration and training for all TK-3 teachers, with a
focus on early literacy development and the implementation of the new
English Language Arts (ELA) curriculum. In addition, a lead teacher team
will receive specialized training on the California Mathematics Framework
to prepare for the 2026-2027 pilot of new math materials.
District staff, program specialists, and instructional coaches will provide
targeted support to ensure effective instructional practices, intervention
strategies, and fidelity of implementation in the classroom. Professional
learning will be designed to strengthen educators’ capacity to meet the
language, literacy, and mathematical learning needs of unduplicated
students, fostering improved academic outcomes. Regular progress
monitoring, coaching, and collaborative planning will ensure that
instructional strategies remain aligned to student needs and evidence-
based best practices.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 1, Metrics 1, 2, 3 and 4.
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $508,745.00
Supplemental & Concentration funds supporting this action: $1,257,353
Reference:
Darling-Hammond, L., Hyler, M. E., & Gardner, M. (2017). Effective
Teacher Professional Development. Palo Alto, CA: Learning Policy
Institute.

actions.title:
Aligned, Evidence-
Based Instructional
Systems
actions.description:
According to the California Department of Education, a Multi-Tiered
System of Support (MTSS) integrates academic, behavioral, and social-
emotional supports within a comprehensive framework to improve
outcomes for all students, particularly English Language Learners, foster
youth, and low-income students (California Department of Education,
2023). With this in mind, during the 2025-2026 school year, USD will
implement a comprehensive MTSS approach in TK-3 that addresses
literacy, mathematics, and social-emotional learning (SEL). This will
include the adoption and implementation of culturally responsive, viable
curriculum aligned to state standards, as well as the administration of local
assessments to monitor student progress in literacy, math, and SEL.
Schools will receive site-based allocations to implement strategies tailored
to the unique needs and context of their student population. These
allocations may include funding for additional staff to provide targeted
interventions, small-group instruction, or individualized support.
Instructional leaders, program specialists, and site administrators will
provide ongoing coaching and progress monitoring to ensure interventions
are data-driven, equitable, and responsive to student needs.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 1, Metrics 1, 2, 3 and 4.
Reference:
California Department of Education. (2023). Multi-Tiered System of
Support (MTSS). Sacramento, CA: California Department of Education.
Retrieved from https://www.cde.ca.gov/ci/cr/ri/

actions.title:
Extended Learning
Opportunities
actions.description:
Provide extended learning opportunities for unduplicated students in TK-3:
* Provide learning opportunities TK-3 during summer/winter/spring
intersession
* Provide extended day learning opportunities for students in TK-3

actions.title:
Equitable & Inclusive
Classrooms
actions.description:
Provide support to assist students in attainment of TK-3 grade-level
standards:
* Provide training and coaching in positive behavior strategies
(Safety Care)
* Reduce class sized beyond the required minimum in grades TK-3,
including additional instructional aide support in the primary
grades.
* Provide additional instructional aide support in the primary
classrooms

actions.title:
Comprehensive and
Inclusive Support for
Students with
Disabilities
actions.description:
Provide targeted support and resources for students with disabilities in TK-
3 to improve academic achievement and prepare them for long-term
success:
* Provide specialized supplemental materials for intervention and
enrichment that address the unique learning needs of students
with disabilities
* Offer professional development for all certificated and classified
staff on evidence-based instructional practices, including
systematic, structured reading, classroom behavior support, and
strategies for differentiating instruction and providing
accommodations for students with disabilities
* Allocate special education staff, such as education specialists and
paraprofessionals, to provide targeted interventions and support
in the classroom
* Utilize data-driven decision-making to identify and monitor the
progress of students with disabilities and provide targeted
interventions based on individual needs
* Provide extended learning opportunities, such as
summer/winter/spring intersession programs and after-school
tutoring, that are specifically designed to address the learning
gaps and build foundational skills for students with disabilities
* Ensure service providers have the knowledge and expertise by
providing professional development focusing on creating
Individualized Education Programs (IEPs) aligned with grade-level
standards that include appropriate goals, accommodations,
modifications, and supports to enable students with disabilities to
access the curriculum and make progress
* Engage families of students with disabilities in the educational
process through regular communication, training, and support to
help them understand their child's learning needs and how to
advocate for their success

actions.title:
Comprehensive and
Inclusive Support for
Long-Term English
Learners
(New Action for Year
2)
actions.description:
Provide professional development in effective instruction for English
language learners:
* Training and implementation of English language development
curriculum to ensure early success of gaining English proficiency
to prevent LTELL status
* Training on instructional strategies to support English learner
access to mastering academic standards

goals.description:
Inclusivity, Safety & Support for All: Each student will experience a supportive, inclusive school
climate that promotes trusting peer and staff relationships, ensures safety, and encourages
confidence.

actions.title:
Culturally Competent
Staff
actions.description:
LREBG Action
The California Department of Education highlights the importance of
Culturally and Linguistically Responsive Teaching, which centers the
cultural and linguistic assets of students-including multilingual students of
color-to make learning more relevant, engaging, and equitable (California
Department of Education, 2025). In alignment with this framework, RUSD
will provide ongoing professional development for all certificated and
classified staff throughout the 2025-2026 school year to strengthen cultural
competency, ensure high expectations for all students, and improve
student-teacher relationships.
Additionally, comprehensive training will be offered to all school personnel
on topics supporting academic, social-emotional, and behavioral learning.
These sessions will include modules on understanding and addressing
implicit bias and promoting inclusive, equity-centered classroom
environments. District leadership, instructional coaches, and mental health
staff will lead and facilitate the training, model evidence-based strategies,
and deliver follow-up coaching to promote transfer into practice.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 2, Metrics 1 and 2.
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $90,000.00
Reference
California Department of Education. (2025, June 5). Culturally and
Linguistically Responsive Teaching. Sacramento, CA: California
Department of Education. Retrieved from
https://www.cde.ca.gov/ci/pl/responsiveteaching.asp

actions.title:
Behavior and Mental
Health Professional
Development
actions.description:
Provide ongoing professional development while monitoring student needs
as related to behaviors, attendance and social emotional learning. Key
actions include:
* Provide training and resources for staff on Positive Behavior and
Support (PBIS)
* Provide social emotional learning (SEL) tools and resources
* Provide ongoing training for site Positive Behavior and Support
(PBIS) teams through district lead and a districtwide Community
of Practice
* Provide data system to monitor PBIS site data at each site
* Expand peer support programs including courses, peer mentoring
programs, peer tutoring programs, and other strategies to build
student to student connection and engagement

actions.title:
Systems to Support
Student Attendance
actions.description:
Districtwide for all Students
* Empathy interviews: site staff uses empathy interviews to build
systems to collect data and feedback to build systems that create
safe and trusting learning environments
* Attendance incentive programs: School leaders build an
environment and maintain processes that support and encourage
families and community participation in school and student
learning
* Site and district leadership meetings: regular collection of
attendance data and monitor progress to ensure inclusive and
equitable success and attendance
* Professional development: ongoing collaborative meetings for
training on attendance procedures with site classified staff
-Tiered attendance plan: implementation of districtwide three-tiered
attendance approach by district and site leadership and support staff
Foster Youth
* Ed Rights student conferencing: district staff provide resources
and shared accountability measures for foster youth through
group student meetings, 1:1 meetings, and home visits to ensure
equitable success
Resource allocation: ensure systems for needs-based allocation of
resources (e.g. transportation) necessary for foster youth success

actions.title: Safety Support
actions.description:
Provide classified staff and ongoing training regarding health and safety
including safe working and learning environments, implementation of
safety plans, and equipment necessary to strengthen response. School
safety staff and noon aides provide additional support for social-emotional
wellbeing on campus for low-income students, English learners and foster
youth. Research indicates that students who feel connected to at least one
supportive adult demonstrate higher success on a range of educational
outcomes. Recent studies even indicate student connectedness has
lasting benefits on their health and well-being (Adolescent Connectedness
and Adult Health Outcomes by Steiner et.al, 2019). Students from poverty
and those living in foster care often experience inconsistent support from
adults outside of school. The additional staffing provided through this
action ensures our unduplicated students have additional contacts
throughout the day to develop strong connections to adults at school.

actions.title:
School Climate
Support
actions.description:
Provide assistance for school climate to support students, including staffing
and resources for the Family Resource Center, social emotional
counseling support at all schools, maintaining five (5) social workers, and
ongoing collaboration with outside agencies to provide mental, social and
emotional support services.

actions.title: Health Services
actions.description:
Provide appropriate health and mental health services to students. Nurses
and health assistants address students’ health needs in school, assist with
vaccination referrals, and promote wellness thereby eliminating barriers
related to health. In order to support the needs of low-income students,
English learners, and foster students, the Rowland Unified School District
funds a Health Assistant at each site. Health Assistants ensure all
students, especially our English language learners, low-income students,
and foster/homeless youth feel safe and healthy at school. They provide
first aide, consultations and communicate with families. Nurses are
available at every school site to provide support to students with diabetic
needs, vaccinations, referrals, consultations and much more. The East
Valley Community Health Care provides neighborhood community clinics
providing medical, dental, and optometry care. They specifically reach out
to English language learners, low-income students and foster/homeless
youth. A strong partnership with East Valley Community Health Care
provides neighborhood community clinics providing medical, dental, and
optometry care when health needs surpass that will health assistants and
nurses can provide. Additional health service (bilingual) provided above the
core to meet the needs of foster students, homeless students, low income
students, and English learners. The Family Resource Center employs
Community Liaisons and Social Workers to work directly with low-income,
foster, and homeless students. Community liaisons facilitate connections to
various agencies including but not limited to counseling/mental health
referrals. Liaisons share resources with parents and support them in
making the best choice they feel will meet the needs of their child. Liaisons
conduct home visits, establish partnerships with motels/homeless shelters
to help identify and support homeless students in RUSD. Social workers
assist all sites with mental health needs and conduct small group
counseling as needed for identified students, particularly McKinney and
Foster populations. The social workers will also supervise interns and
provide training to FRC personnel as needed.

actions.title: High Quality Staff
actions.description:
Recruit, hire, retain and promote the highest quality staff for all positions in
the District.

actions.title:
Leadership
Professional
Development
actions.description:
Provide professional development and growth opportunities for District
leaders to engage in continuous improvement efforts, including RUSD
leadership institutes and academies for certificated and classified staff, and
outside professional development opportunities.

actions.title:
Facilities and
Technology
Infrastructure
Maintenance
actions.description:
Utilize the Routine Restricted Maintenance Account to ensure facilities are
in good repair, including an upgrade to outdoor learning and play spaces to
address the needs of all students. Maintain technology infrastructure to
support 21st Century Teaching and Learning, including broadband access
at school, and wifi hotspots for all students who need access at home.
Update outdoor environments including updating playgrounds in
elementary schools and adding additional water fountains throughout
campuses.

actions.title:
Inclusive Support for
Students with
Disabilities
actions.description:
Provide targeted support and resources for students with disabilities to
improve academic achievement and prepare them for long-term success:
* Offer comprehensive training to all school personnel on topics
related to special education, such as understanding Individualized
Education Programs (IEPs), implementing accommodations and
modifications, and fostering an inclusive school culture
* Provide targeted support and resources for students with
disabilities to promote positive behavior, attendance, and social-
emotional learning
* Provide ongoing training and support for special education staff
and paraprofessionals on evidence-based practices, data
collection, and progress monitoring to ensure the effectiveness of
interventions and supports for students with disabilities
* Offer professional development and growth opportunities for
district leaders and special education administrators to enhance
their knowledge and skills in supporting inclusive education and
improving outcomes for students with disabilities

actions.title:
Inclusive Support for
Long-Term English
Learners
actions.description:
Provide ongoing support for Long-Term English learners through the
following:
* Embed culturally responsive teaching practices for secondary
LTELs into ongoing training around integrated ELD
* Expand restorative practices and ensure consistent
implementation of behavioral expectations across school sites to
support LTELs’ social-emotional well-being and school belonging.

goals.description:
Student Agency, Advocacy & Empowerment: Each student will develop the skills to effectively
communicate their needs and be proactive to take ownership of the learning process.

actions.title: Student Voice
actions.description:
Provide structures for student voice and choice through site-based and
districtwide two-way communication with focus groups, surveys, monthly
student board reports, and a student voice advisory council comprised of
students in grade 5-12 to understand what students need throughout the
year. Develop expanded structures for students to participate in district
decision-making.

actions.title:
Staff and Community
Voice
actions.description:
Provide opportunities for collaboration between the school district and all
educational partners.

actions.title: Parent Education
actions.description:
RUSD will provide parent education to support student learning and
achievement, including topics addressing core instruction areas of
mathematics and literacy, technology, social emotional strategies. The
training will include RUSD Family Connect which provides training by
Parent/Guardian leaders across the district, along with continued training
developed and provided by Special Projects designed to meet the needs of
parents of English learner parents.

actions.title: Translation Services
actions.description:
C o mmunicate in appropriate languages with families, including the
provision of district staff translators in Spanish and Chinese, including
translation of additional languages through contracted services.

actions.title:
Family
Communication
actions.description:
Provide information to parents and community through Parent Square, the
District and school site websites, the RUSD mobile application, RUSD E-
news, print and Social Media.

actions.title:
Inclusive Support for
Long-Term English
Learners
actions.description:
Ensure participation and engagement of LTELs in district decision making
through the following:
* Conduct empathy interviews and districtwide focus groups to
center LTEL experiences in decision-making.
* Strengthen counselor engagement with LTELs to support
academic and social-emotional growth.
* Provide parent engagement opportunities for families of LTELs to
support academic progress of LTELs.

goals.description:
Equitable Access to Academic Success: Each student will have access to resources and support to
demonstrate growth toward and achievement of rigorous academic standards.

actions.title:
Curriculum and
Materials
actions.description:
Access to high-quality, standards-aligned instructional materials across all
subject areas is a foundational element of educational equity and
academic success, particularly for English Language Learners, foster
youth, and low-income students (California Department of Education,
2023). In alignment with this principle, USD will provide comprehensive,
standards-aligned curriculum and instructional materials in grades 4-12,
including English Language Arts, Mathematics, Science, Social Studies,
World Language, Visual and Performing Arts, and Electives.
During the 2025-2026 school year, the district will engage in the adoption
cycle for 7-12 World Language and 9-12 Health to ensure alignment with
current standards and relevance to students’ lived experiences.
Additionally, USD will provide supplemental materials designed to support
both academic intervention and enrichment, ensuring differentiated access
to rigorous learning opportunities.
To support literacy development and student engagement, school sites will
also be provided with library materials tailored to student interests and
reading levels, along with a centralized library tracking system to improve
access and equity. Educational Services staff, curriculum leads, and site
administrators will monitor implementation and gather feedback from
educators and students to ensure materials meet the academic and
cultural needs of unduplicated students.
Reference
California Department of Education. (2023). Instructional Materials:
Standards and Frameworks. Sacramento, CA: California Department of
Education. Retrieved from https://www.cde.ca.gov/ci/cr/cf/

actions.title:
Responsive and
Aligned Professional
Development
actions.description:
LREBG Action
Research consistently shows that sustained, job-embedded professional
development focused on evidence-based instructional practices
significantly improves teacher effectiveness and student outcomes,
especially for English Language Learners, Long-Term English Learners,
foster youth, and low-income students (Darling-Hammond et al., 2017;
Saunders, Goldenberg, & Marcelletti, 2013). In alignment with this
research and the California Department of Education’s emphasis on high-
leverage professional learning strategies (CDE, 2023), RUSD will
implement a comprehensive professional development plan for all
certificated and classified staff during the 2025-2026 school year.
This action includes targeted training for teachers on instructional
strategies that support language acquisition and address the unique
academic and linguistic needs of Long-Term English Learners. District
staff, including program specialists and instructional coaches, will provide
support implementation at school sites, and offer intervention strategies.
Ongoing site-based professional learning time will be embedded into the
school calendar to allow educators to analyze student data, reflect on
instructional practices, and collaboratively plan lessons designed to close
opportunity gaps. Additionally, cross-site collaboration and articulation time
will be provided to foster shared learning and continuity of supports for
students across campuses and grade spans.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 4, Metrics 1, 2, 3 and 4.
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $736,025
Supplemental & Concentration funds supporting this action: $15,517,028
Federal funds supporting this action $543,398
References
California Department of Education. (2023). Professional Learning and
Support. Sacramento, CA: California Department of Education. Retrieved
from https://www.cde.ca.gov/pd/ps/
Darling-Hammond, L., Hyler, M. E., & Gardner, M. (2017). Effective
Teacher Professional Development. Palo Alto, CA: Learning Policy
Institute.
Saunders, W., Goldenberg, C., & Marcelletti, D. (2013). English Language
Development: Guidelines for Instruction. American Educator, 37(2), 24-31.

actions.title:
Aligned, Evidence-
Based Instructional
Systems
actions.description:
LREBG Action
A well-implemented Multi-Tiered System of Support (MTSS) is recognized
as a powerful framework to address the academic, behavioral, and social-
emotional needs of students, particularly English Language Learners,
foster youth, and low-income students (California Department of
Education, 2023; McIntosh & Goodman, 2016). In alignment with this
research and state guidance, USD will expand its MTSS approach in the
2025-2026 school year to ensure all sites provide responsive and data-
informed support across tiers 1, 2, and 3.
Schools will receive targeted support for developing and implementing
universal instruction (Tier 1), targeted group interventions (Tier 2), and
intensive individualized support (Tier 3), with a focus on the specific needs
of unduplicated students. Site-based collaboration opportunities will be
embedded throughout the year to facilitate progress monitoring, share best
practices, and continuously refine intervention systems. To ensure
sufficient capacity, additional staff will be allocated to provide academic
and social-emotional interventions, including structures for credit recovery
and grade span transitions.
RUSD will also continue to provide students with 24/7 access to academic
tutoring, both in-person and virtual, to increase equitable learning time and
close opportunity gaps. Special attention will be given to meeting the
needs of students in the Alternative Learning Program, ensuring that they
have access to tiered supports, transition plans, and learning recovery
resources tailored to their unique contexts.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 4, Metrics 1, 2, 3 and 4
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $1,244,114
Federal funds supporting this action: $16,932
Supplemental & Concentration funds supporting this action:$996,851
References
California Department of Education. (2023). Multi-Tiered System of
Support (MTSS). Sacramento, CA: California Department of Education.
Retrieved from https://www.cde.ca.gov/ci/cr/ri/
McIntosh, K., & Goodman, S. (2016). Integrated Multi-Tiered Systems of
Support: Blending RTI and PBIS. New York, NY: Guilford Press.

actions.title:
Extended Learning
Opportunities
actions.description:
LREBG Action
Extended learning opportunities have been shown to accelerate learning,
support credit recovery, and mitigate learning gaps for historically
underserved students, including English Language Learners, foster youth,
and low-income students (Kidron & Lindsay, 2014; California Department
of Education, 2023). In alignment with this research and state priorities,
USD will provide extended learning opportunities for unduplicated students
in grades 4-12 throughout the 2025-2026 school year.
This includes access to credit recovery courses to support on-time
graduation, as well as academic programming during summer, winter, and
spring intersessions to extend learning time beyond the traditional school
year. Extended day learning opportunities will also be offered at school
sites, including tutoring, enrichment activities, and academic support
tailored to student need. In addition, students will have access to 24/7
tutoring services, both in-person and virtual, to support homework
completion, skill reinforcement, and subject-specific interventions.
These extended learning supports are provided districtwide to ensure
equitable access to supplemental instructional time and academic recovery
opportunities, regardless of school site, ultimately improving outcomes for
unduplicated students.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 4, Metrics 1, 2, and 4
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $106,250
State funds supporting this action: $841,426
Supplemental & Concentration funds supporting this action:$1,307,216
References
California Department of Education. (2023). Expanded Learning
Opportunities Program. Sacramento, CA: California Department of
Education. Retrieved from https://www.cde.ca.gov/ls/ex/
Kidron, Y., & Lindsay, J. (2014). The Effects of Increased Learning Time
on Student Academic and Nonacademic Outcomes: Findings from a Meta-
Analytic Review. U.S. Department of Education, Institute of Education
Sciences, Regional Educational Laboratory Appalachia.

actions.title:
Supporting Core
Instruction for English
Learners / Long-
Term English
Learners
actions.description:
LREBG Action
Research consistently shows that English learners, particularly Long-Term
English Learners (LTELs), benefit from sustained academic support that
includes both high-quality core instruction and targeted interventions
aligned to their language development needs (Olsen, 2010). With this in
mind, during the 2025-2026 school year, RUSD will provide
comprehensive support to assist English learners in attaining grade-level
standards. This includes instructional academic support within Tier 1 and
Tier 2 frameworks, with a focus on improving outcomes for LTELs in both
language and content areas.
District staff will provide additional services designed to support English
learners during key transitions from elementary to middle and from middle
to high school, ensuring accurate placement and academic planning.
Designated and Integrated English Language Development (ELD)
instruction will be supported through professional development for
teachers, aligned to the California ELD Standards. Coaching and
collaboration will build teacher capacity to differentiate instruction and
promote academic discourse in both ELD and core content areas. In
addition, the district will expand learning opportunities for LTELs through
after-school and summer programs, with targeted supports focused on
academic language development, grade span transitions, and scaffolding
to access rigorous curriculum.
This action is designed to meet the specific needs of English learners and
LTELs while also enhancing teacher effectiveness and systemwide
coherence in language development practices.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 1, Metrics 1, 2, and 3
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $10,000
Supplemental & Concentration funds supporting this action: $600,262
Reference:
Olsen, L. (2010). Reparable Harm: Fulfilling the Unkept Promise of
Educational Opportunity for California’s Long Term English Learners. Long
Beach, CA: Californians Together.

actions.title:
Data-informed
Progress Monitoring
and Culture of
Continuous
Improvement
actions.description:
Support maintenance and use of data systems:
-Providing student information system to house student demographic and
outcome data
-Provide assessment systems to appropriately identify student needs and
monitor effectiveness of interventions
-Provide survey platform to collect ongoing input from educational partners
-Provide ongoing training and support for implementing continuous
improvement cycles
-Provide support on using multiple measures to improve student
achievement

actions.title: Site-based Actions
actions.description:
S upport site-based actions to close the achievement gap for English
learners, low-income students, and foster youth. Actions are included in
their Single Plan for Student Achievement and aligned with LCAP goals.
Site actions fall into the following categories:
-Provide Instructional coaches that bring evidence-based practices into
classrooms and assist teachers in differentiating instruction for targeted
students
-Provide additional professional development on the effective integration of
technology into instruction
-Provide specialized programs that assist students in college preparedness
-Provide specialized programs that assist students with mental health
-Provide specialized programs to close the achievement gap

actions.title:
Enrichment and
Beyond the Core
Instruction
actions.description:
Provide resources and programs enrichment and beyond the core
programs:
* Provide resources for academic competitions
* Provide resources for athletics
* Provide resources for International Baccalaureate programs
* Provide resources and staffing for music education
* Provide learning opportunities for GATE students

actions.title: Staffing
actions.description:
Provide appropriate staffing to support student learning, including
certificated and classified staff.

actions.title:
English Language
Acquisition Programs
actions.description:
Provide English Language Development: Designated English Language
D e velopment (ELD) is a protected time during the regular school day when
teachers use the CA ELD Standards as the focal standards in ways that
build into, and from, content instruction to develop the critical English
language skills, knowledge, and abilities needed for content learning in
English. During this protected time, ELs are actively engaged in
collaborative discussions in which they build awareness of language and
develop skills and abilities to use language. During Designated ELD , there
is a strong emphasis on academic oral language development. Students
receive differentiated curriculum that are aligned to the English Language
Development Standards The final step in providing effective EL instruction
is differentiating instruction by proficiency level to meet students’ needs,
which follows appropriate scheduling and flexible grouping of ELs for
instructional purposes.

actions.title:
English Language
Acquisition
Professional
Development
actions.description:
Professional development for English language development, both
Integrated English Language Development (ELD) and Designated ELD, is
critical for teachers’ effective implementation of research based strategies
for instructing English Learners in these settings. Professional
development for English Learners utilizes research based approaches
guided by the Common Core Standards and English Language
Development Standards Professional development and coaching
opportunities for teachers with focused conversations around best
practices for language progression. Through collaborative practices such
as coaching, lesson study, teachers observe and practice research based
strategies. The training is presented in four distinct parts to allow teachers
to attend the training, take time to apply strategies, analyze and monitor
their students, and reflect on their practice as teachers.

actions.title:
Inclusive
Comprehensive
Support for Academic
Success of Students
with Disabilities
(To address
requirements for
Differentiated
Assistance)
actions.description:
Provide comprehensive targeted support to improve academic outcomes
for students with disabilities:
* Provide ongoing professional development for all staff on
evidence-based instructional practices, differentiation strategies,
and accommodations to support the academic success of
students with disabilities in all subject areas
* Allocate special education staff, such as education specialists and
paraprofessionals, to provide targeted interventions and support
in the classroom across all grade levels
* Offer extended learning opportunities tailored to the needs of
students with disabilities, including specialized
summer/winter/spring intersession programs, after-school
tutoring, and virtual learning resources
* Collaborate with the Alternative Learning Program to develop
inclusive and accessible learning pathways for students with
disabilities who may benefit from non-traditional educational
settings
* Utilize data systems and assessment tools to regularly monitor the
progress of students with disabilities, inform instructional
decisions, and evaluate the effectiveness of interventions and
support services
* Engage in ongoing collaboration and professional development
with special education staff to ensure the successful
implementation of inclusive practices and differentiated instruction
across all grade levels

goals.description:
Future Ready: Each student will successfully transition to and graduate from high school with the
essential skills and knowledge for the successful pursuit of the future of their choice, including
college and career.

Future Ready
Learning
actions.description:

middle and high school engage in the development of their Future Ready
plan to foster students’ growth and learning aligned to students’ needs and
interests. Expand implementation of career interest inventory for all
secondary students.
-Provide districtwide staff to provide professional development on

actions.title:
College and Career
Programs
actions.description:
LREBG Action
College and career readiness is a critical component of educational equity,
particularly for English Language Learners, foster youth, and low-income
students, who often face systemic barriers to postsecondary access and
success (Conley, 2010; California Department of Education, 2023). In
response, RUSD will implement a comprehensive, developmentally
appropriate college and career program for grades 4-12 to provide early
exposure, planning tools, and direct experiences that prepare students for
a range of postsecondary pathways.
During the 2025-2026 school year, the district will continue developing a
vertically articulated college and career exploration program that scaffolds
awareness, readiness, and planning from elementary through high school.
All elementary and secondary schools will provide college and career field
trips to expose students to postsecondary institutions, industries, and
training opportunities. Students in grades 7-12 will engage with an online
Career Interest Platform (i.e. YouScience) to explore careers, research
colleges, and establish postsecondary goals aligned with their interests
and aptitudes.
College and Career Centers will be maintained at all intermediate and high
schools to offer support with academic planning, financial aid, college
applications, and career exploration. Additionally, the AVID (Advancement
Via Individual Determination) program will be offered at Hollingworth,
Oswalt, Alvarado, Giano, Nogales High School, and Rowland High School
to support students with academic habits, higher education access, and
college-going mindsets. RUSD will also strengthen community
partnerships to offer local and regional internships, job-shadowing, and
work-based learning experiences that connect students with real-world
pathways.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 5, Metrics 1, 2, and 3.
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $45,542.00
Supplemental & Concentration funds supporting this action: $499,735
Federal funds supporting this action: $140,643
References
California Department of Education. (2023). College/Career Indicator
(CCI). Sacramento, CA: California Department of Education. Retrieved
from https://www.cde.ca.gov/ta/ac/cm/
Conley, D. T. (2010). College and Career Ready: Helping All Students
Succeed Beyond High School. San Francisco, CA: Jossey-Bass.

actions.title:
College and Career
Readiness for Foster
Youth, Low-Income
Students, and
Students
Experiencing
Homelessness
actions.description:
LREBG Action
Students in foster care, experiencing homelessness, or living in poverty
face significant systemic barriers to college and career readiness, including
instability in housing, disrupted schooling, and limited access to counseling
and guidance resources. Research shows that targeted supports-
including individualized college planning, financial aid assistance, and
sustained mentorship-can significantly improve postsecondary access
and success for these student groups (Unrau et al., 2012; California
Department of Education, 2023). In response, RUSD will implement a set
of strategic supports designed to increase college and career readiness
among foster youth, low-income students, and students experiencing
homelessness during the 2025-2026 school year.
The “College Connect” program will provide one-on-one support to
students navigating the college application process, including guidance on
completing the FAFSA and identifying appropriate postsecondary
opportunities. College information and awareness workshops will be
tailored specifically to the needs and lived experiences of students in foster
care, low-income households, and those experiencing homelessness, with
individualized follow-up support to reinforce understanding and promote
college-going outcomes.
RUSD will also provide expanded counseling services to ensure these
students receive the personalized support needed to build postsecondary
plans, select appropriate college and/or career prep courses, access
interventions as needed, and monitor progress through graduation.
Additionally, the district will maintain robust procedures and data
infrastructure to ensure students in foster care, experiencing
homelessness, or involved with the juvenile court system are promptly
identified, placed in appropriate educational programs, awarded partial
credits when applicable, and supported throughout their transitions
between schools.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 5, Metrics 1, 2, and 3
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $15,000.00
Supplemental & Concentration funds supporting this action:$1,482,254
Federal funds supporting this action:$16,263
References
California Department of Education. (2023). Foster Youth Services
Coordinating Program (FYSCP). Sacramento, CA: California Department
of Education. Retrieved from https://www.cde.ca.gov/ls/pf/fy/
Unrau, Y. A., Font, S. A., & Rawls, G. (2012). Readiness for College
Engagement Among Students Who Have Aged Out of Foster Care.
Children and Youth Services Review, 34(1), 76-83.
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.childyouth.2011.09.002

actions.title:
College and Career
Readiness for
English learners,
including Long-Term
English Learners
actions.description:
LREBG Action
English learners, and particularly Long-Term English Learners (LTELs),
often face unique barriers to college and career readiness due to limited
access to rigorous academic coursework, inconsistent language
development support, and lack of culturally and linguistically responsive
college preparation opportunities (Olsen, 2010; California Department of
Education, 2023). To address these challenges, RUSD will implement
targeted programs and supports during the 2025-2026 school year to
accelerate learning and promote equitable access to postsecondary
pathways for English learners.
RUSD will implement specialized structures and programs designed to
accelerate academic progress and college readiness for LTELs, including
strategic course placement and scaffolded instruction aligned to A-G and
college-preparatory requirements. Targeted tutoring will be provided by
personnel trained to meet the academic and linguistic needs of English
learners, supported by an online platform that reinforces essential
concepts in language arts and mathematics through adaptive, language-
aware design.
To build the academic confidence and college-going identity of middle
school English learners, the district will provide the AVID Excel program at
Alvarado Intermediate and Giano Intermediate, with a specific focus on
developing academic vocabulary, organizational strategies, and access to
college-prep coursework. Additionally, RUSD will offer college information
and awareness workshops designed specifically for English learners, with
individual follow-up support. All materials and sessions will be available in
the preferred language of families, including those of LTELs, to ensure full
understanding and engagement in the college and career planning
process.
Metric being used to monitor the action: Goal 5, Metrics 1, 2, and 3.
LREBG Funds supporting this action: $31,000.00
References
California Department of Education. (2023). English Learner Roadmap
Policy. Sacramento, CA: California Department of Education. Retrieved
from https://www.cde.ca.gov/sp/el/rm/
Olsen, L. (2010). Reparable Harm: Fulfilling the Unkept Promise of
Educational Opportunity for California’s Long Term English Learners. Long
Beach, CA: Californians Together.

actions.title:
Inclusive Pathways to
College and Career
Readiness for
Students with
Disabilities
actions.description:
Provide targeted support and inclusive opportunities for college and career
readiness:
* Provide professional development for staff on Universal Design for
Learning (UDL) principles and strategies to create inclusive
classrooms that meet the diverse needs of all learners, including
students with disabilities
* Allocate additional counseling support for students with disabilities
to identify post-secondary college/career plans, ensure enrollment
in appropriate college and/or career prep courses, and monitor
progress until graduation
* Ensure students with disabilities have equitable access to college
and career curriculum, including STEM courses and Career
Technical Education (CTE) pathways, by providing necessary
accommodations, modifications, and support
* Develop and implement inclusive college and career exploration
programs, field trips, and workshops that are tailored to the needs
of students with disabilities
* Provide targeted support for students with disabilities in the
College Connect program, including assistance with financial aid
applications, college research, essay writing, and application
requirements, considering their individual needs and
accommodations
* Partner with postsecondary institutions, employers, and
community organizations to develop inclusive transition programs,
internships, and work experiences for students with disabilities,
promoting their success in college and career
* Offer specialized workplace learning opportunities for students
with disabilities, such as community-based experiences, service
learning, and workplace mentoring, to develop job skills and
support their transition to post-secondary employment
* Provide professional development for staff on strategies to support
the successful inclusion of students with disabilities in A-G
courses, dual enrollment programs, and CTE pathways, ensuring
they receive the necessary accommodations and support to meet
course requirements
* Collaborate with families of students with disabilities through the
Family Connect program to engage them in the education
process, provide information and resources, and support their
child's college and career readiness
* Utilize data systems to monitor the progress and outcomes of
students with disabilities in college and career readiness
indicators, such as A-G completion, CTE pathway completion,
and SBAC performance, to inform targeted interventions and
support

goals.description:
Equity Multiplier Goal for Santana High School (SHS) and Rowland Virtual Learning Academy
(RVLA). The focus of SHS equity multiplier goal is on equipping staff and students with design
thinking, cross-disciplinary project approaches, and 21st-century skills to support real-world problem
solving, civic engagement, and effective project implementation. The strategies for Rowland Virtual
Learning Academy focus on applying design thinking and evidence-based practices to real-world
challenges while integrating 21st-century leadership, SEL skills, and cross-disciplinary project
planning into both professional development and student learning.

Equity Multiplier
Action
actions.description:
Key actions for Santana High School include:
-Learn the design thinking process and explore evidence-based
approaches to applying it to authentic real world challenges in professional
roles and student curricular activities.
-Experience sample cross-disciplinary project examples that integrate
design thinking, social entrepreneurship, and civic engagement.
-Introduce tools and strategies to integrate intentional 21st century
leadership and SEL skill instruction into professional development and
classroom instruction, including collaboration, problem-solving,
communication, and project
management.
-Begin planning for project implementation incorporating new frameworks
and strategies.
